Text:
The features below are not available in New Game+. To use them, please suspend your progress and return to standard game mode.

■Certain features accessed through NPCs and objects:
・Market boards
・Hunt boards
・Clan Hunt boards
・Summoning bells
・Inn room features
・Weather forecasts
・Unattuned aetherytes and Aethernet shards
・Duties accessible from NPCs
・All leves
・Triple Triad
・Gold Saucer games
・Housing-related features
・Grand Company features
・Free Company features
・The Hall of the Novice
・Eternal Bonding ceremonies
・Certain delivery moogles
・Certain vendors
・Certain menders
・Interaction with certain objects
・Conversation with certain NPCs

■Certain features accessed through item use:
・Use of Tales of Adventure items

The features below are not available in New Game+. To use them, please suspend your progress and return to standard game mode.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rules■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A Frontline alliance consists of 24 members, divided into 3 full parties. There are 3 alliances, which combine for a total of 72 participants.
A match lasts for 20 minutes.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their Grand Company's landing, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay. This will not incur a weakness penalty.
Furthermore, players will be temporarily invulnerable when leaving their Grand Company's landing.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Mounts■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Players are free to use mounts during Frontline campaigns. However, players taking damage while mounted will be afflicted with the Limp status effect.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Return■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Using Return will teleport players back to their landing point. Moreover, this action has no recast time during Frontline campaigns. Please note, however, casting will be interrupted by attacks.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■KDA■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each time a player incapacitates an enemy player, they are awarded a knockout (K). When a player is incapacitated, they are classed as “down” (D). If a player deals damage to an enemy that is then incapacitated by a teammate, they are awarded an assist (A). The final K, D, and A for each player is displayed on the results screen at the end of the battle.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rewards■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When a match has ended, players will receive PvP EXP, PvE EXP, Wolf Marks, and Allagan tomestones.
By continually winning Frontline matches, players will receive achievements that reward special mounts and gear.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Damage Modifiers■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Damage dealt and damage taken in Frontline campaigns are adjusted based on your current job. For specific adjustment values, please visit the Frontline Play Guide on the Lodestone.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rules■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A Frontline alliance consists of 24 members, divided into 3 full parties. There are 3 alliances, which combine for a total of 72 participants.
A match lasts for 20 minutes.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their Grand Company's landing, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay. This will not incur a weakness penalty.
Furthermore, players will be temporarily invulnerable when leaving their Grand Company's landing.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Mounts■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Players are free to use mounts during Frontline campaigns. However, players taking damage while mounted will be forced off and afflicted with the Hoofing It status effect for five seconds.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Return■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Using Return will teleport players back to their landing point. Moreover, this action has no recast time during Frontline campaigns. Please note, however, casting will be interrupted by attacks.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■KDA■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each time a player incapacitates an enemy player, they are awarded a knockout (K). When a player is incapacitated, they are classed as “down” (D). If a player deals damage to an enemy that is then incapacitated by a teammate, they are awarded an assist (A). The final K, D, and A for each player is displayed on the results screen at the end of the battle.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rewards■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When a match has ended, players will receive PvP EXP, PvE EXP, Wolf Marks, and Allagan tomestones.
By continually winning Frontline matches, players will receive achievements that reward special mounts and gear.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Damage Modifiers■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Damage dealt and damage taken in Frontline campaigns are adjusted based on your current job. For specific adjustment values, please visit the Frontline Play Guide on the Lodestone.

Key locations such as outposts and Allagan ruins can be identified by flags, indicating the Grand Company that currently controls them. At the start of each match, all locations will be unoccupied. By remaining in close proximity to the flags at these locations, players can occupy them to improve their team's tactical rating. The location will become occupied more quickly when you have a higher number of team members near the flag than the opposing alliances. The greater the difference in players, the quicker it will be occupied. However, these areas may turn neutral should enemies come within proximity of a flag controlled by your team, or vice versa.

Key locations such as outposts and Allagan ruins can be identified by flags, indicating the Grand Company that currently controls them. At the start of each match, all locations will be unoccupied. By remaining in close proximity to the flags at these locations, players can occupy them to improve their team's tactical rating.

Every three seconds, tactical points will be accrued as follows:
　・When occupying one key location, an increase of 2
　・When occupying two key locations, an increase of 4
　・When occupying three key locations, an increase of 6
　・When occupying four key locations, an increase of 10
　・When occupying five key locations, an increase of 12
　・When occupying six key locations, an increase of 14
　・When occupying seven key locations, an increase of 18
　・When occupying eight key locations, an increase of 20
　・When occupying nine key locations, an increase of 22

While standing in key locations, players will be buffed with the status Sheer Will, greatly increasing damage dealt. The location will become occupied more quickly when you have a higher number of team members near the flag than the opposing alliances. The greater the difference in players, the quicker it will be occupied. However, these areas may turn neutral should enemies come within proximity of a flag controlled by your team, or vice versa.

■Keyboard and Mouse
All duty actions and items can be used by left-clicking their icon, but can be bound to other keys.

■Gamepad
Duty actions are used by pressing <CommandIcon(17)/> while holding down both <CommandIcon(14)/> and <CommandIcon(15)/>. Lost actions from the item category must be selected from the lost finds holster. They may also be assigned to your hotbar.

■Keyboard and Mouse
All duty actions and items can be used by left-clicking their icon, but can be bound to other keys.

■Gamepad
Duty actions are used by pressing <Gui(17)/> while holding down both <CommandIcon(14)/> and <CommandIcon(15)/>. Lost actions from the item category must be selected from the lost finds holster. They may also be assigned to your hotbar.

Selecting this button will open the Outfit Glamour Creation window. Here you can select items from your Inventory and Armory Chest to include in the outfit glamour. Gear with condition below 100% cannot be used to form an outfit glamour.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Unlike standard glamours, the color of dyed gear is not transferred to an outfit glamour.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

※Outfit glamours can still be dyed after being projected onto gear or added to a glamour plate.

When creating an outfit glamour, several items are combined as an ensemble and saved to the glamour dresser.

Selecting this button will open the Outfit Glamour Creation window. Here you can select items from your Inventory and Armoury Chest to include in the outfit glamour. Gear with condition below 100% cannot be used to form an outfit glamour.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Unlike standard glamours, the color of dyed gear is not transferred to an outfit glamour.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

※Outfit glamours can still be dyed after being projected onto gear or added to a glamour plate.

When creating an outfit glamour, several items are combined as an ensemble and saved to the glamour dresser.
